focus: Enumerate the complete set of flat child-relative leaf-ID collisions across all five child routers and validate the packet-qualified coordinate scheme.
noveltyJustification: 4 of 5 findings were fully new and 1 partially new (0.90), plus a 0.10 simplicity bonus for resolving the canonical-coordinate contradiction.
keyQuestions: Which flat child-relative leaf IDs collide, and what packet-qualified coordinate scheme removes ambiguity?
answeredQuestions: Which flat child-relative leaf IDs collide, and what packet-qualified coordinate scheme removes ambiguity?
ruledOut: Flat child-relative leaf IDs are globally unique
Packet-qualified leaf ID alone is a complete routing identity
Canonical leafResourceId should embed the packet prefix

label: Five routers expose 88 packet-local coordinates but 79 flat IDs, with exactly eight colliding IDs.
Seven collisions span research and review; convergence_signals also spans AI Council.
Packet qualification makes all 88 packet-local coordinates globally unique.
The implemented sk-doc typed pair is workflowMode plus packet-local leafResourceId; packet is manifest metadata.
Packet-only qualification cannot distinguish the three workflow modes sharing deep-improvement.

direction: Flat child-relative leaf IDs are globally unique
reason: Eight exact IDs collide across packet routers.

direction: Packet-qualified leaf ID alone is a complete routing identity
reason: Three public workflow modes share deep-improvement.

direction: Canonical leafResourceId should embed the packet prefix
reason: The existing contract keeps leafResourceId packet-local and treats packet-qualified strings as legacy input.
